title: iD Editor
description: A user-friendly, web-based map editor for OpenStreetMap, designed for beginners and experienced mappers alike.

title: JOSM
description: Java OpenStreetMap Editor (JOSM) is a powerful desktop application for editing OpenStreetMap data.

title: MapWithAI
description: MapWithAI is a web-based tool that leverages artificial intelligence to assist in the editing and enhancement of OpenStreetMap data.

title: OsmAnd
description: OsmAnd is a map and navigation application for Android and iOS that uses OpenStreetMap data.

title: OSMCha
description: OSMCha is a web-based tool for reviewing changes in OpenStreetMap data, helping to ensure data quality and integrity.

title: Vespucci
description: Vespucci is an advanced OpenStreetMap editor for Android, offering a wide range of features suitable for experienced mappers.

title: Go Map!!
description: Go Map!! is a feature-rich OpenStreetMap editor for iOS, allowing users to add and edit map data directly from their iPhone or iPad.

title: RapiD
description: RapiD is an enhanced version of the iD editor that integrates AI-suggested map features to accelerate OpenStreetMap contributions.

title: Overpass API
description: Overpass API is a powerful read-only API that lets you query and retrieve specific OpenStreetMap data using a custom query language.

title: Overpass Turbo
description: Overpass Turbo is a web-based data filtering and extraction tool for OpenStreetMap, running Overpass API queries and visualising results on a map.

title: Taginfo
description: Taginfo provides statistics and documentation about the keys and tags used in OpenStreetMap, helping mappers understand tagging conventions.

title: OSM Inspector
description: OSM Inspector is a web-based data quality analysis tool maintained by Geofabrik that highlights potential errors and inconsistencies in OpenStreetMap data.

title: ohsome API
description: The ohsome API enables users to analyse the evolution of OpenStreetMap history data, providing aggregation endpoints for temporal and spatial analysis.

title: Maputnik
description: Maputnik is a free and open source visual map designer and editor for the Mapbox GL Style Specification, supporting OSM-based tile sources.

title: MapLibre GL JS
description: MapLibre GL JS is a community-led open source fork of Mapbox GL JS that renders interactive vector tile maps using WebGL.

title: OpenLayers
description: OpenLayers is a high-performance open source library for rendering interactive maps in web browsers, supporting OpenStreetMap tile layers.

title: Leaflet
description: Leaflet is a lightweight, mobile-friendly JavaScript library for building interactive maps, widely used with OpenStreetMap tile layers.

title: Mapnik
description: Mapnik is an open source toolkit for developing mapping applications that renders the standard OpenStreetMap tile layer used on openstreetmap.org.

title: Osmium Tool
description: Osmium Tool is a versatile command-line tool based on the Osmium library for processing OSM files, supporting merge, filter, extract, and export operations.

title: pyosmium
description: pyosmium is a Python library providing bindings to the Osmium library, enabling efficient processing of OpenStreetMap data files in Python.

title: Osmosis
description: Osmosis is a Java command-line application and library for processing OSM data, capable of filtering, transforming, and loading data into databases.

title: osmtogeojson
description: osmtogeojson converts OpenStreetMap data to GeoJSON, making it easy to use OSM data in web mapping applications and data analysis workflows.

title: OSRM
description: Open Source Routing Machine (OSRM) is a high-performance routing engine for shortest paths in road networks, built on OpenStreetMap data.

title: Valhalla
description: Valhalla is an open source routing engine and accompanying libraries for creating turn-by-turn navigation applications using OpenStreetMap data.

title: OpenRouteService
description: OpenRouteService is an open source route planner offering directions, isochrones, and matrix services based on OpenStreetMap data.

title: Organic Maps
description: Organic Maps is a privacy-focused, ad-free offline maps app for Android and iOS built on OpenStreetMap data, ideal for hiking, cycling, and navigation.

title: Maps.me
description: Maps.me is an offline maps and navigation app for Android and iOS that uses OpenStreetMap data, allowing navigation without an internet connection.

title: GeoServer
description: GeoServer is an open source server for sharing, processing, and editing geospatial data, supporting OpenStreetMap data via PostGIS and WMS/WFS services.

title: QGIS
description: QGIS is a free and open source geographic information system that supports OSM data import, editing, and analysis via its QuickOSM plugin and native data sources.

title: Geofabrik Download Server
description: Geofabrik's download server provides regularly updated regional extracts of OpenStreetMap data in PBF and OSM XML formats for easy download.

title: BBBike Extract
description: BBBike's OSM extract service lets users download custom-area OpenStreetMap data in various formats including Shapefile, GeoJSON, CSV, and more.

title: Nominatim
description: Nominatim is the official geocoding and reverse geocoding service for OpenStreetMap, allowing structured address searches and coordinate lookups.

title: Photon
description: Photon is an open source geocoder built for OpenStreetMap data, powered by Elasticsearch and offering fast, typo-tolerant address and place-name search.

title: OpenMapTiles
description: OpenMapTiles provides an open source solution for self-hosting vector tiles derived from OpenStreetMap data, compatible with MapLibre and other renderers.

title: GraphHopper
description: A fast, open source routing engine and web service that builds customizable route networks directly from OpenStreetMap data.

title: BRouter
description: An advanced routing engine primarily designed for cyclists and hikers, utilizing elevation profiles alongside OSM data.

title: uMap
description: An open source web tool that allows users to create custom, interactive maps with OpenStreetMap layers and data overlays.

title: Osmose
description: A quality assurance tool for OpenStreetMap that detects spatial and logical issues to assist mappers in fixing data errors.

title: Keep Right
description: A data consistency checker for OpenStreetMap that highlights potential geometry and tagging errors on a live map interface.

title: Mapillary
description: A street-level imagery platform that captures, shares, and extracts map data using computer vision, heavily integrated with OSM editing.

title: KartaView
description: A free and open source street-level imagery platform aimed at improving OpenStreetMap by providing crowdsourced visual reference data.

title: Level0
description: A web-based OpenStreetMap editor that lets users manipulate raw OSM data quickly using a lightweight, text-based interface.

title: Merkaartor
description: An open source OpenStreetMap desktop editor built using the Qt framework, providing robust tools for tracking and editing offline.

title: Pelias
description: A modular, open source geocoder built on Elasticsearch that works natively with OpenStreetMap data and other open spatial datasets.

title: osm2pgsql
description: A standard command-line utility for importing OpenStreetMap data into a PostgreSQL/PostGIS database for rendering and advanced analysis.

title: Imposm
description: A fast and efficient tool to import OpenStreetMap data into PostGIS databases, highly optimized for web map rendering environments.

title: Protomaps
description: A system for self-hosted interactive vector maps using a single file (PMTiles), working seamlessly with OpenStreetMap derived datasets.

title: Magic Earth
description: A privacy-focused mobile navigation app providing turn-by-turn routing, 3D maps, and offline support powered by OpenStreetMap.

title: Komoot
description: A popular route planning and navigation app for outdoor activities, utilizing OpenStreetMap data for detailed trail and topography routing.

title: Field Papers
description: A web-based tool allowing users to print paper maps, annotate them in the field, and upload them back for tracing into OSM.

title: OSM Buildings
description: A web library and viewer for visualizing 3D building geometry derived from OpenStreetMap data on interactive web maps.

title: F4Map
description: A 3D interactive map utilizing OpenStreetMap data, WebGL, and weather effects to render cities, buildings, and topography globally.

title: Show Me The Way
description: A real-time visualization tool that displays the latest OpenStreetMap edits from around the world as they happen on an interactive map.

title: WhoDidIt
description: A quality assurance and history tool that allows users to analyze edits within a specific bounding box and identify active contributors.

title: Bounding Box Tool
description: A simple web utility for retrieving the bounding box coordinates of an area, essential for constructing OSM queries and API requests.

title: Traccar
description: A modern open source GPS tracking system that supports real-time location tracking and integrates OpenStreetMap for its mapping interface.

title: Kurviger
description: A motorcycle route planner that prefers curvy and scenic roads, powered by OpenStreetMap data and tailored specifically for riders.

title: GeoDesk
description: A fast and lightweight spatial database toolkit specifically built for querying and analyzing OpenStreetMap data natively.

title: Waymarked Trails
description: A thematic map viewer that displays hiking, cycling, riding, and skating route networks worldwide directly generated from OSM data.

title: Potlatch
description: A classic desktop editor for OpenStreetMap, previously built as a web app but now available as a standalone desktop application for Mac and Windows.

title: OpenSeaMap
description: An open source project providing free nautical charts, beacons, and maritime information globally, functioning as a specialized layer built on OpenStreetMap data.

title: OpenCycleMap
description: A specialized global map layer tailored for cyclists, highlighting cycle routes, dedicated lanes, elevation contours, and bicycle-friendly amenities using OSM data.

title: OpenRailwayMap
description: A detailed, interactive map of the world's railway infrastructure, visualizing tracks, signals, maximum speeds, and stations natively derived from OpenStreetMap.

title: OpenInfraMap
description: A visual representation of global infrastructure, illustrating power lines, sub-stations, telecommunications, and petroleum networks mapped within the OSM database.

title: Thunderforest
description: A commercial provider offering a suite of specialized OpenStreetMap tile layers, including Outdoors, Transport, and Landscape maps tailored for developers.

title: MapTiler
description: A mapping platform offering scalable vector tiles, map hosting, and highly customizable map styles powered by OpenStreetMap and other open datasets.

title: OpenCage Geocoder
description: An enterprise-grade geocoding API that aggregates OpenStreetMap and other open geo-datasets to provide reliable forward and reverse geocoding worldwide.

title: How Did You Contribute (HDYC)
description: A widely used profiling tool that provides comprehensive statistics, heatmaps, and visual activity charts for any registered OpenStreetMap user account.

title: OSMStats
description: A daily updated statistics dashboard that tracks overall database growth, active contributors, and editing trends across the global OpenStreetMap community.

title: ResultMaps
description: A QA toolset by Pascal Neis that visualizes unmapped places, recent community activity, and OpenStreetMap data quality issues on interactive maps.

title: Maperitive
description: A desktop application used to draw customizable maps in real-time from raw OSM data, frequently used for creating high-quality print maps and SVG exports.

title: Tangram
description: An open source WebGL map rendering engine originally created by Mapzen, capable of rendering highly styled 2D and 3D maps from OpenStreetMap vector tiles.

title: OpenSnowMap
description: A dedicated web map for winter sports enthusiasts, displaying ski pistes, cross-country trails, and ski lift networks using crowd-sourced OSM data.

title: Navit
description: An open source car navigation system with a routing engine that dynamically renders vector maps from OpenStreetMap data without requiring an internet connection.

title: CycleStreets
description: A UK-centric bicycle journey planning system utilizing OpenStreetMap data to provide tailored routing options for cyclists of all experience levels.

title: Pic4Carto
description: A web-based tool that aggregates open street-level imagery from multiple platforms to seamlessly assist remote mappers in OpenStreetMap tracing.

title: osm2po
description: A routing engine and converter that processes OpenStreetMap data into topology-aware routing graphs suitable for pgRouting and standalone navigation servers.

title: Addok
description: A fast, customizable search engine primarily designed for geocoding, backed by Redis and built specifically to natively support OpenStreetMap data imports.

title: MapProxy
description: An open source tile caching proxy for geospatial data that accelerates map delivery, capable of serving OpenStreetMap tiles to desktop and web GIS applications.

title: Turf.js
description: An advanced geospatial analysis library for browsers and Node.js, heavily used within the OpenStreetMap ecosystem for processing GeoJSON data on the fly.

title: RouteXL
description: A multi-stop route planner utilizing OpenStreetMap data and advanced traveling salesperson algorithms to find the most optimal itinerary for deliveries and logistics.

title: OpenHistoricalMap
description: A sister project to OpenStreetMap dedicated to creating a universal, crowd-sourced map of the world's history across different time periods using the OSM software stack.
